displayTitle: Flat Product, Flat Flaws, Straight Beam

beats.narration: Rolling doesn't erase an ingot's leftovers — it flattens them. Plate, sheet, and the laminations a straight beam was born to find.

payload.title: FLATTENED FLAWS
payload.sub: Rolling squeezes ingot leftovers into laminations.

beats.narration: Unhealed pipe and blowholes flatten into thin laminations. Inclusion clusters smear into stringers. Nearly everything lies parallel to the surfaces, near mid thickness.

payload.heading: What rolling does to ingot flaws

beats.narration: Straight beam, broadside hit. A lamination echo stands near half thickness, the back wall drops away, and multiples crowd in at half spacing.

payload.heading: The straight-beam lamination signature
payload.note: ECHO AT T/2 · NO BACK WALL · HALF SPACING

beats.narration: Tight or open, steel to air reflects nearly all the sound. Map it: walk the probe to the six decibel drop, record depth and extent.
Here's the rule: flat product, flat flaws, straight beam. Clear the plate first, and every angle beam downstream can be trusted.

payload.sub: Clear the scanning zone before angle-beam work.
